visa_types.name: Visa For Spouse/Parent/Children Of Eu /German Citizen
visa_types.documents_required:
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/qat/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Please take note that data in the application form and the scope and type of documents supporting the application are demanded by the staff of VFS upon request of the German Embassy!
Applicants are advised to prepare their applications as per the document checklist on VFS website. As per instructions from German Embassy, VFS will not accept applications if complete set of documents as per checklist  are not provided on the day of appointment.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person (12 years of age and above) at the time of submission of visa application.
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                  documents
Instructions for applicants below 18 years
- For all children below 18years of age, both parents must be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the VIDEX and supporting forms.
SCHENGEN VISA – for applicants with GERMAN spouse, parents of minor German citizens or minor children of German citizens
SCHENGEN VISA for applicants with EU spouse parent or child
Declaration of Consent/Parental Authorization
List of Health Insurance
Letter of Guarantee for accompanying worker
Letter of Guarantee for relatives (Husband-Wife-Son –Daughter)
General Instruction Form
visa_types.visa_fees:
[deu > qat > en > Table:Tourist Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge per application over and above visa fees. Fees can be paid in cash or by debit/credit card
- All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for the application at the German Embassy is approximately 15 days.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences
visa_types.forms:
Below is the form that is required to be filled to submit your visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt) (To be filled online - mandatory)
The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled in English.As per the below sample :
Specimen Visa Application Form
Instructions to complete an online form: 
- Click on the form link above
- When the page opens complete the form
- Once complete print out the form on clear paper
- The complete printed set of your application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application.
** The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine readable format.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The Visa Application Centre in Qatar will only accept Visa Applications from citizens of Qatar or persons holding residency issued by Qatar and must be staying in Qatar as a resident for consecutive 6 months. The Embassy of the Federal Republic of Germany represents the following
                                  Schengen countries in Qatar for processing Schengen visas: Austria, Finland and Slovenia.
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person (12 years of age and above) at the time of submission.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The Visa Application Centre in Qatar will only accept Visa Applications from citizens of Qatar or persons holding residency issued by Qatar and must be staying in Qatar as a resident for consecutive 6 months. The Embassy of the Federal Republic of Germany represents the following
                                  Schengen countries in Qatar for processing Schengen visas: Austria, Finland and Slovenia.
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person (12 years of age and above) at the time of submission.

## Visa Fees

[deu > qat > en > Table:Tourist Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge per application over and above visa fees. Fees can be paid in cash or by debit/credit card
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/qat/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Please take note that data in the application form and the scope and type of documents supporting the application are demanded by the staff of VFS upon request of the German Embassy!
Applicants are advised to prepare their applications as per the document checklist on VFS website. As per instructions from German Embassy, VFS will not accept applications if complete set of documents as per checklist  are not provided on the day of appointment.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person (12 years of age and above) at the time of submission of visa application.
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                  documents
Instructions for applicants below 18 years
- For all children below 18years of age, both parents must be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the VIDEX and supporting forms.
SCHENGEN VISA – for applicants with GERMAN spouse, parents of minor German citizens or minor children of German citizens
SCHENGEN VISA for applicants with EU spouse parent or child
Declaration of Consent/Parental Authorization
List of Health Insurance
Letter of Guarantee for accompanying worker
Letter of Guarantee for relatives (Husband-Wife-Son –Daughter)
General Instruction Form

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
Click here to view a sample biometric photo

Requirements:
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-26mm) of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application at the German Embassy is approximately 15 days.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Download Forms
Below is the form that is required to be filled to submit your visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt) (To be filled online - mandatory)
The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled in English.As per the below sample :
Specimen Visa Application Form
Instructions to complete an online form: 
- Click on the form link above
- When the page opens complete the form
- Once complete print out the form on clear paper
- The complete printed set of your application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application.
** The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine readable format.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/qat/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Please take note that data in the application form and the scope and type of documents supporting the application are demanded by the staff of VFS upon request of the German Embassy!
Applicants are advised to prepare their applications as per the document checklist on VFS website. As per instructions from German Embassy, VFS will not accept applications if complete set of documents as per checklist  are not provided on the day of appointment.
Accompanying domestic workers :- Sponsor must be present at VFS to sign the guarantee form in front of VFS Staff and present original QID and Passport
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person (12 years of age and above) at the time of submission of visa application.
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                  documents
Instructions for applicants below 18 years
- For all children below 18years of age, both parents must be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the VIDEX and supporting forms.
Document Checklist 
Declaration of Consent/Parental Authorization
List of Health Insurance
Letter of Guarantee for accompanying worker
Letter of Guarantee for relatives (Husband-Wife-Son –Daughter)
General Instruction Form
visa_types.visa_fees:
[deu > qat > en > Table:Tourist Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge per application over and above visa fees. Fees can be paid in cash or by debit/credit card
- All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
Processing time for visa applications will take up to 15 days according to the Schengen Visa Codex.
 Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned maximum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
 Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance (up to 180 days before planned departure) to avoid any inconveniences

Below is the form that is required to be filled to submit your visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt) (To be filled online - mandatory)
The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled in English.As per the below sample :

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The Visa Application Centre in Qatar will only accept Visa Applications from citizens of Qatar or persons holding residency issued by Qatar and must be staying in Qatar as a resident for consecutive 6 months. The Embassy of the Federal Republic of Germany represents the following
                                  Schengen countries in Qatar for processing Schengen visas: Austria, Finland and Slovenia.
For Persons applying for Airport Transit Visa
Entry clearance requirements depend on the citizenship and resident status of the applicant (sometimes in a third country). Only a flight through one Schengen Union Country at a time
                                  is considered as a transit. The vast majority of travelers do not need a visa when air transiting through Germany. In Germany, the following airports have an International Transit
                                  Area: Cologne/Bonn (transit only possible between 4:30 am and 11:00 pm), Düsseldorf (transit only possible between 6:00 am and 9:00 pm and only if the airline has arranged the transit
                                  with airport security – please check with your airline), Frankfurt/Main, Hamburg (limited time frame only 4:30 am to 11:30 pm), Munich (München).
Please check with your travel agent / airline, if your flight is going via another airport. If you leave the airport’s international transit area, i.e. change the terminal, you
                                  will have to apply for a Schengen Visa – Category C.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The Visa Application Centre in Qatar will only accept Visa Applications from citizens of Qatar or persons holding residency issued by Qatar and must be staying in Qatar as a resident for consecutive 6 months. The Embassy of the Federal Republic of Germany represents the following
                                  Schengen countries in Qatar for processing Schengen visas: Austria, Finland and Slovenia.
For Persons applying for Airport Transit Visa
Entry clearance requirements depend on the citizenship and resident status of the applicant (sometimes in a third country). Only a flight through one Schengen Union Country at a time
                                  is considered as a transit. The vast majority of travelers do not need a visa when air transiting through Germany. In Germany, the following airports have an International Transit
                                  Area: Cologne/Bonn (transit only possible between 4:30 am and 11:00 pm), Düsseldorf (transit only possible between 6:00 am and 9:00 pm and only if the airline has arranged the transit
                                  with airport security – please check with your airline), Frankfurt/Main, Hamburg (limited time frame only 4:30 am to 11:30 pm), Munich (München).
Please check with your travel agent / airline, if your flight is going via another airport. If you leave the airport’s international transit area, i.e. change the terminal, you
                                  will have to apply for a Schengen Visa – Category C.

## Visa Fees

Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.

Documents required Non Qatar Nationals for Transit Visa 

Declaration of Consent/Parental Authorization
List of Health Insurance
Letter of Guarantee for accompanying worker
Letter of Guarantee for relatives (Husband-Wife-Son –Daughter)
General Instruction Form

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
Click here to view a sample biometric photo

Requirements:
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-26mm) of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form

The processing time for the application at the German Embassy is approximately 15 days.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The Visa Application Centre in Qatar will only accept Visa Applications from citizens of Qatar or persons holding residency issued by Qatar and must be staying in Qatar as a resident for consecutive 6 months. The Embassy of the Federal Republic of Germany represents the following
                                  Schengen countries in Qatar for processing Schengen visas: Austria, Finland and Slovenia.
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required. 
Under the Business visa category the following cases would be applicable:
- Business matters/meetings/ negotiation/trainings
- Conference/Congress/Exhibition
- Scientific/Research events
- Journalists
- Official delegations

## Visa Fees

[deu > qat > en > Table:Tourist Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge per application over and above visa fees. Fees can be paid in cash or by debit/credit card
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/qat/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Please take note that data in the application form and the scope and type of documents supporting the application are demanded by the staff of VFS upon request of the German Embassy!
Applicants are advised to prepare their applications as per the document checklist on VFS website. As per instructions from German Embassy, VFS will not accept applications if complete set of documents as per checklist  are not provided on the day of appointment.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person (12 years of age and above) at the time of submission of visa application.
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                  documents
Instructions for applicants below 18 years
- For all children below 18years of age, both parents must be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the VIDEX and supporting forms.

Document Checklist 
Declaration of Consent/Parental Authorization
List of Health Insurance
Letter of Guarantee for accompanying worker
Letter of Guarantee for relatives (Husband-Wife-Son –Daughter)
General Instruction Form

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
Click here to view a sample biometric photo

Requirements:
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-26mm) of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application at the German Embassy is approximately 15 days.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Download Forms
Below is the form that is required to be filled to submit your visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt) (To be filled online - mandatory)
The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled in English.As per the below sample :
Specimen Visa Application Form
Instructions to complete an online form: 
- Click on the form link above
- When the page opens complete the form
- Once complete print out the form on clear paper
- The complete printed set of your application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application.
** The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine readable format.

visa_types.name: SCHENGEN VISA, CATEGORY C
visa_types.overview:
Schengen Rule
The visa application has to be presented to the Embassy/Consulate of the country of main destination (determined by the length of stay).
If your stay is equal in all Schengen states, the Schengen state through which you first enter the territory of these countries is responsible for granting the visa.
The Visa Application Centre in Qatar will only accept Visa Applications from citizens of Qatar or persons holding residency issued by Qatar and must be staying in Qatar as a resident for consecutive 6 months. The Embassy of the Federal Republic of Germany represents the following Schengen countries in Qatar for processing Schengen visas: Austria, Finland and Slovenia.
Do I need a Visa for Germany?
Click here (http://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/EN/EinreiseUndAufenthalt/StaatenlisteVisumpflicht_node.html) to check if you require a visa to enter Germany.
Schengen Visa and Visa Code
In order to apply for a Schengen visa, the applicant needs to provide the documents as per the purpose of the trip. In individual cases, additional documents may be requested. Presentation of the documents does not automatically lead to the issuance of a visa and possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa.The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.A Visa can be applied for as early as 180 days before the expected date of departure.
The Visa Code standardizes the visa requirements which must be examined by the mission in the course of the visa procedure. The respective mission makes a decision on the visa application at its own discretion, taking into account all the circumstances in any given individual case.
All visa applicants for Germany are advised to schedule an appointment and visit the Joint Visa Application Centre in Doha to submit their applications, together with all necessary documents
Processing time for a visa may take up to 15 days. Applicants are therefore requested to organise themselves accordingly taking into consideration possible waiting periods due to the online appointment system.
The purpose of the proposed visit may not be altered once the visa has been issued. This is also applicable after entering the Schengen territory.
Above is regular information, if you wish to continue and to know more about the Visa application process, kindly click here.
Information on this website is subject to change, kindly check the information for updates before you apply.
visa_types.full_text:
Schengen Rule
The visa application has to be presented to the Embassy/Consulate of the country of main destination (determined by the length of stay).
If your stay is equal in all Schengen states, the Schengen state through which you first enter the territory of these countries is responsible for granting the visa.
The Visa Application Centre in Qatar will only accept Visa Applications from citizens of Qatar or persons holding residency issued by Qatar and must be staying in Qatar as a resident for consecutive 6 months. The Embassy of the Federal Republic of Germany represents the following Schengen countries in Qatar for processing Schengen visas: Austria, Finland and Slovenia.
Do I need a Visa for Germany?
Click here (http://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/EN/EinreiseUndAufenthalt/StaatenlisteVisumpflicht_node.html) to check if you require a visa to enter Germany.
Schengen Visa and Visa Code
In order to apply for a Schengen visa, the applicant needs to provide the documents as per the purpose of the trip. In individual cases, additional documents may be requested. Presentation of the documents does not automatically lead to the issuance of a visa and possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa.The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.A Visa can be applied for as early as 180 days before the expected date of departure.
The Visa Code standardizes the visa requirements which must be examined by the mission in the course of the visa procedure. The respective mission makes a decision on the visa application at its own discretion, taking into account all the circumstances in any given individual case.
All visa applicants for Germany are advised to schedule an appointment and visit the Joint Visa Application Centre in Doha to submit their applications, together with all necessary documents
Processing time for a visa may take up to 15 days. Applicants are therefore requested to organise themselves accordingly taking into consideration possible waiting periods due to the online appointment system.
The purpose of the proposed visit may not be altered once the visa has been issued. This is also applicable after entering the Schengen territory.
Above is regular information, if you wish to continue and to know more about the Visa application process, kindly click here.
Information on this website is subject to change, kindly check the information for updates before you apply.

visa_types.processing_time: The Embassy will review your application and decide whether the criteria to issue a visa are met. Depending on the purpose of your stay and as consultation with administrative bodies in Germany is necessary, processing your visa application may take up to three months. As soon as the process is finalized, the Embassy will inform you about the decision. During this period you cannot inquire about the status of your application. Upon expiry of the processing period only the applicant, his/her legal representative or  persons holding an authorization letter from the applicant may inquire about the status of the visas.

## Overview
The Visa Application Centre in Qatar will only accept Visa Applications from citizens of Qatar or persons holding residency issued by Qatar and must be staying in Qatar as a resident for consecutive 6 months.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of application submission.
Instructions for applicants below 18 years
- For all children below 18 years of age, both parents must be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the VIDEX and supporting forms.

## Visa Fees

[deu > qat > en > Table:Student Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in QAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge per application over and above visa fees. Fees can be paid in cash or by debit/credit card
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please take note that data in the application form and the scope and type of documents supporting the application are demanded by the staff of VFS upon request of the German Embassy!
Applicants are advised to prepare their applications as per the document checklist on VFS website. As per instructions from German Embassy, VFS will not accept applications if complete set of documents as per checklist  are not provided on the day of appointment.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission of visa application.
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                  documents
Instructions for applicants below 18 years
- For all children below 18years of age, both parents must be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the VIDEX and supporting forms.
Documents required for student visa 
Declaration of Consent/Parental Authorization
General Instruction Form

## Processing Time
The Embassy will review your application and decide whether the criteria to issue a visa are met. Depending on the purpose of your stay and as consultation with administrative bodies in Germany is necessary, processing your visa application may take up to three months. As soon as the process is finalized, the Embassy will inform you about the decision. During this period you cannot inquire about the status of your application. Upon expiry of the processing period only the applicant, his/her legal representative or  persons holding an authorization letter from the applicant may inquire about the status of the visas.

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
Click here to view a sample biometric photo

- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-26mm) of the photo

- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form

visa_types.name: NATIONAL VISA CATEGORY D
visa_types.overview:
VFS is authorised to accept visa applications for student visa category only. Student visa applicants will have to schedule appointments to submit their applications through VFS website. 
All the other categories for national or long stay visas (Category D) for Germany are handled directly by the Embassy of Germany, Doha, Qatar, applications can be submitted only with prior appointments.
The Visa Application Centre in Qatar will only accept Visa Applications from citizens of Qatar or persons holding residency issued by Qatar and must be staying in Qatar as a resident for consecutive 6 months.
The German Embassy is only allowed to issue your visa, if a confirmation has been issued by the Foreign Nationals Authority in Germany(ausländerbehörde).. You will be informed automatically when
                            the residence permit has been authorized. The German Embassy is committed to keep the processing time to a minimum.Applicants are requested to apply at least 3 months prior to their intended
                            start of the visa in order to ensure the completion of the process and to avoid disappointment
A Visa can be applied for as early as 180 days before the start of the student course. Your visa can only be issued to you from Doha,Qatar. It cannot be forwarded to Germany and it cannot be issued in Germany after your arrival. After your arrival in Germany you must register as soon
                            as possible with the Foreign Nationals Authority of your place of residence
visa_types.full_text:
VFS is authorised to accept visa applications for student visa category only. Student visa applicants will have to schedule appointments to submit their applications through VFS website. 
All the other categories for national or long stay visas (Category D) for Germany are handled directly by the Embassy of Germany, Doha, Qatar, applications can be submitted only with prior appointments.
The Visa Application Centre in Qatar will only accept Visa Applications from citizens of Qatar or persons holding residency issued by Qatar and must be staying in Qatar as a resident for consecutive 6 months.
The German Embassy is only allowed to issue your visa, if a confirmation has been issued by the Foreign Nationals Authority in Germany(ausländerbehörde).. You will be informed automatically when
                            the residence permit has been authorized. The German Embassy is committed to keep the processing time to a minimum.Applicants are requested to apply at least 3 months prior to their intended
                            start of the visa in order to ensure the completion of the process and to avoid disappointment
A Visa can be applied for as early as 180 days before the start of the student course. Your visa can only be issued to you from Doha,Qatar. It cannot be forwarded to Germany and it cannot be issued in Germany after your arrival. After your arrival in Germany you must register as soon
                            as possible with the Foreign Nationals Authority of your place of residence

## Information and Application Forms:
For Information leaflets and application forms please click  here (http://www.doha.diplo.de/Vertretung/doha/en/07-Visabestimmungen/02-National-Visa/National-Visa-15032017.html)
For long stay visa appointments except student visa with Embassy please click  here (https://doha.diplo.de/qa-en/service/visa/nationalvisa/1671436)
